Title: Revision Workflow Watermarking & On-Set Supervisor
Description: Deliver four capabilities that bridge script authoring and physical production: (1) the Revision Color Workflow — industry-standard 13-colour WGA revision sequence with locked version management, per-recipient watermarked PDF distribution, and scene-level diff views; (2) the Watermark Service — steganographic watermark embedding in PDF (character spacing) and FDX (zero-width characters), callable by the revision-distribution Temporal saga; (3) Call Sheet generation — Temporal saga that assembles call sheet data, generates a PDF, routes for AD approval, and distributes to crew; and (4) the Supervisor Module + PowerSync Offline — Supervisor Service for take logging and continuity assertions, PowerSync cloud sync, and the Tauri 2.x on-set desktop app with SQLite + SQLCipher encryption. At the end of Sprint 9 the Beta milestone countdown begins: 3 production teams must be operational by Sprint 11. Reference: Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprints 9 and 10, Wiki/06-offline-sync.md, Wiki/11-security-compliance.md.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Revision color state machine: 13 colours per WGA spec; locked pages reject colour change
- Watermarked PDF round-trip: embed recipient ID → extract from leaked copy → recover correct recipient
- Revision distribution saga: correct watermarked PDF delivered to each recipient via email
- Call sheet PDF generated and distributed to 3 test crew members
- Tauri app runs on macOS; script supervisor logs takes offline for 1 hour; all sync on reconnect
- SQLCipher encryption verified: SQLite DB file unreadable without key
- PowerSync sync works for supervisor and AD roles (different data subsets)
- Supervisor Service deployed to staging with passing integration tests
- Beta milestone target set: 3 production teams to onboard by Sprint 11 end
Tags: revision; watermark; call-sheet; supervisor; tauri; powersync; sprint-9; sprint-10

Title: Revision Color Workflow and Version Distribution Saga
Description: Implement the WGA-standard 13-colour revision sequence as a state machine in the Script Service. Locked versions are immutable — pages cannot change colour after lock. The revision-distribution Temporal saga generates per-colour PDFs, embeds per-recipient forensic watermarks, and distributes via email. Writers can compare any two locked versions using the scene-level diff endpoint. Reference: Wiki/03-data-model.md §5, Wiki/07-workflow-orchestration.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 9.
Acceptance Criteria:
- 13-colour state machine enforces valid transitions; locked pages return 400 on colour change attempt
- scriptVersionDiff returns scene-level unified diff between any two version snapshots
- Revision distribution saga completes; each recipient receives correct watermarked PDF
- Version history panel shows all locked versions with colour badge and diff access
Parent: Revision Workflow Watermarking & On-Set Supervisor

Title: Watermark Service — Steganographic Embedding and Forensic Extraction
Description: Build the Watermark Service to embed invisible recipient-identifying watermarks in exported PDFs (character spacing steganography) and in FDX exports (zero-width Unicode characters between dialogue). The embedWatermarkPerRecipient gRPC method is called by the revision-distribution saga. The extraction endpoint allows the security team to identify the source of a leak. Reference: Wiki/11-security-compliance.md, ADR-025, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 9.
Acceptance Criteria:
- PDF watermark: embed recipient ID → extract from leaked PDF → recover correct recipient ID in extraction test
- FDX watermark: zero-width chars encode recipient ID; extraction recovers ID from FDX string
- embedWatermarkPerRecipient gRPC callable from revision-distribution saga; returns S3 key of watermarked file
- Extraction endpoint: POST /watermark/extract with PDF upload returns { recipientId, confidence }
Parent: Revision Workflow Watermarking & On-Set Supervisor

Title: Call Sheet Saga — Generation Approval and Distribution
Description: Implement the Call Sheet Temporal saga that assembles call sheet data from the Scheduling, Supervisor, and Script services, generates a print-quality PDF following industry standard layout, routes it to the 1st AD for approval, and distributes to crew assignments on approval. Reference: Wiki/07-workflow-orchestration.md, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 9.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Call sheet PDF matches industry standard layout (production name, date, scenes, cast, crew, logistics)
- AD approval step integrated in Temporal saga; call sheet held until approved
- Distribution: email to all crew assignments on approval
- Call sheet viewer renders in web app with print layout
Parent: Revision Workflow Watermarking & On-Set Supervisor

Title: Supervisor Service and PowerSync Offline Sync
Description: Build the Supervisor Service (PostgreSQL, gRPC, GraphQL subgraph) for take logging, continuity assertions, and deviation tracking. Configure PowerSync cloud sync with role-based data routing for supervisor and AD roles. Build the Tauri 2.x on-set desktop app with SQLite + WAL + SQLCipher encryption, PowerSync Rust SDK integration, Tauri commands for take logging, sync status indicator, and scene list UI. Reference: Wiki/06-offline-sync.md, ADR-003, ADR-008, Wiki/15-roadmap.md Sprint 10.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Supervisor Service gRPC: logTake, rateTake, createContinuityAssertion, getPresignedUploadURL all passing integration tests
- NATS events: supervisor.take.circled and supervisor.scene.completed published correctly
- PowerSync sync works for supervisor and AD roles (different data subsets)
- Tauri app runs on macOS; SQLCipher DB file unreadable without key
- Offline test: log 50 takes offline → reconnect → all 50 appear in cloud Postgres within 60s
Parent: Revision Workflow Watermarking & On-Set Supervisor

Title: As a writer I can track coloured revision pages following the WGA standard sequence and compare any two locked versions with a scene-level diff
Description: Industry-standard colour revisions are required for WGA-covered productions. The 13-colour sequence (White → Blue → Pink → Yellow → Green → Goldenrod → Buff → Salmon → Cherry → Tan → Sage → Lilac → White 2nd) must be enforced as a state machine. Locked page colours cannot be changed. Reference: Wiki/03-data-model.md §5.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Colour badge (Blue, Pink, Yellow…) visible in script header; changes via dropdown
- Invalid transition (e.g. jumping from White to Pink when Blue is next) returns error: 'Expected next colour: Blue'
- Locked pages reject colour change: 'Page is locked — colour cannot be changed'
- scriptVersionDiff(fromId, toId) returns: added scenes (green), removed scenes (red), modified scenes (amber)
- Version history panel: list of locked versions sorted by lock date; click opens diff view
Parent: Revision Color Workflow and Version Distribution Saga

Title: As a distribution manager I can distribute colour-revised scripts with per-recipient invisible watermarks so leaks can be traced to the source
Description: Screenplay leaks are a serious problem for productions. Every exported copy must carry an invisible forensic watermark that identifies the specific recipient. Character spacing steganography in PDF and zero-width characters in FDX provide deniable-plausible watermarks that survive printing and scanning. Reference: Wiki/11-security-compliance.md, ADR-025.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Export modal shows 'Recipient watermark will be embedded' warning before download
- Each exported PDF has a unique invisible watermark per recipient
- Given a leaked PDF: POST /watermark/extract returns { recipientId, confidence > 0.95 }
- Revision distribution saga: correct watermarked file sent to each crew member email address
- Watermark survives: PDF print → photograph → PDF re-scan (steganography robustness test)
Parent: Watermark Service — Steganographic Embedding and Forensic Extraction

Title: As a script supervisor I can log takes and continuity assertions offline during a full shooting day and sync to cloud when connectivity returns
Description: The on-set environment is unreliable — sets are in basements, remote locations, and signal-dead zones. The Tauri on-set app must work offline for a full 12–16 hour shooting day with no degradation of functionality. PowerSync provides the offline-first sync layer. SQLCipher protects sensitive production data at rest. Reference: Wiki/06-offline-sync.md, ADR-003.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Tauri app launches and functions fully with no network connection
- logTake, rateTake, createContinuityAssertion, attachContinuityPhoto all work offline
- SQLCipher: DB file is binary garbage without the derived key (verified via hex dump)
- Sync status indicator: shows pending write count and 'OFFLINE' badge when disconnected
- After reconnect: all offline takes and assertions appear in cloud Postgres within 60s
- Photo uploads: enqueued offline; uploaded to S3 on reconnect; S3 key written back to SQLite
Parent: Supervisor Service and PowerSync Offline Sync

Title: As a 1st AD I can view continuity assertion conflicts detected when two devices circle different takes for the same scene and resolve them manually
Description: Concurrent on-set devices are common in large productions. If two script supervisors both circle different takes for the same scene, a conflict is created and must be resolved by the 1st AD before sync completes. Reference: Wiki/06-offline-sync.md conflict detection section.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Conflict detected when two devices submit circle for same scene with different take numbers
- Conflict visible in a pending conflicts list in both Tauri app and web dashboard
- 1st AD selects the authoritative circle; resolution syncs to all devices
- Resolution creates an audit log entry with actor and timestamp
Parent: Supervisor Service and PowerSync Offline Sync

Title: Implement revision color state machine with 13-colour WGA sequence and locked-page enforcement in Script Service
Description: In services/script/src/revision/color-state-machine.ts implement RevisionColorStateMachine. WGA sequence constants: WHITE → BLUE → PINK → YELLOW → GREEN → GOLDENROD → BUFF → SALMON → CHERRY → TAN → SAGE → LILAC → WHITE_2ND. Method transitionColor(currentColor, newColor): RevisionColorResult. Validates: newColor must be the next colour in sequence OR the same (no change). Returns { allowed, nextExpectedColor }. If not allowed: throw InvalidColorTransitionError('Expected next revision colour: [colour]'). Method lockPage(pageId): marks page as color_locked = true in script.ast_nodes. Locked pages reject any updateRevisionColor call with FAILED_PRECONDITION 'Page is locked'. In Script Service GraphQL schema: mutation updateRevisionColor(scriptId, color): Script!; mutation lockRevisionPages(scriptId): Int! (returns count of locked pages). Unit tests: assert all 13 valid transitions; assert invalid transition throws; assert locked page rejects update.
Parent: As a writer I can track coloured revision pages following the WGA standard sequence and compare any two locked versions

Title: Implement scriptVersionDiff gRPC method returning scene-level unified diff between two script version snapshots
Description: In services/script/src/grpc/script.service.ts implement ScriptVersionDiff(req: { fromVersionId, toVersionId }): DiffResult. Algorithm: (1) Load from and to version snapshots from script_versions (CRDT snapshot or AST flattening). (2) Build scene list for each version: { sceneId, headingText, contentHash }. (3) Compute scene-level diff using Myers diff algorithm on contentHash arrays. (4) For changed scenes: compute word-level diff on scene text using diff-match-patch library. (5) Return DiffResult: { addedScenes: SceneRef[], removedScenes: SceneRef[], modifiedScenes: SceneDiff[], unchangedCount: number }. SceneDiff: { sceneId, headingText, wordDiff: [{ op: 'insert'|'delete'|'equal', text }] }. Expose via GraphQL: scriptVersionDiff(fromVersionId, toVersionId): ScriptDiff!. Integration test: create two script versions with one scene added and one modified; assert diff shows both changes.
Parent: As a writer I can track coloured revision pages following the WGA standard sequence and compare any two locked versions

Title: Implement revision distribution saga Temporal worker that generates per-recipient watermarked PDFs and distributes via email
Description: In workers/revision-dist/src/workflows/revision-dist.workflow.ts implement RevisionDistributionWorkflow. Steps: (1) FetchRecipientsActivity: query script distribution list for this script version; return { recipients: { userId, email, name }[] }. (2) GenerateBasePDFActivity: call Script Service export endpoint to generate base PDF from script version (Courier 12pt, correct margins, revision colour headers). (3) ForEachRecipient (parallel): WatermarkActivity: call WatermarkService.embedWatermarkPerRecipient(pdfS3Key, recipientId) gRPC → returns watermarkedS3Key. (4) EmailDeliveryActivity: call Notification Service to send email with S3 presigned download URL (48h expiry). (5) LogDistributionActivity: write distribution_log row with { scriptVersionId, recipientId, watermarkedS3Key, sentAt }. Compensation: if email delivery fails for a recipient, mark that row as failed and continue for others (partial failure allowed). Integration test: run saga with 3 test recipients; assert 3 watermarked PDFs in S3; assert 3 emails dispatched.
Parent: As a distribution manager I can distribute colour-revised scripts with per-recipient invisible watermarks

Title: Implement PDF character spacing steganographic watermark embedding and extraction in Watermark Service
Description: In services/watermark/src/pdf/pdf-watermark.ts implement PdfWatermark class using pdf-lib library. Embedding algorithm: (1) Encode recipientId (UUID) as a 128-bit binary string. (2) Map bits to character spacing modifiers: bit 0 → default spacing (0em), bit 1 → +0.002em spacing (imperceptible visually, detectable by algorithm). (3) Traverse all text objects in the PDF; apply spacing to word spaces in action and dialogue text (skip scene headings to preserve formatting). (4) Return modified PDF bytes. Extraction algorithm: reverse process — traverse PDF text objects; measure word spacing deltas; decode bit string; recover UUID. Method embedWatermark(pdfBytes: Buffer, recipientId: UUID): Promise<Buffer>. Method extractWatermark(pdfBytes: Buffer): Promise<{ recipientId: UUID | null, confidence: float }>. Confidence: ratio of correctly decoded bits. Integration test: embed UUID in 10-page test PDF; extract; assert recipientId matches; assert confidence > 0.95.
Parent: As a distribution manager I can distribute colour-revised scripts with per-recipient invisible watermarks

Title: Implement FDX zero-width character steganographic watermark and build embedWatermarkPerRecipient gRPC method
Description: In services/watermark/src/fdx/fdx-watermark.ts implement FdxWatermark class. Zero-width character encoding: use Unicode zero-width space (U+200B) for bit 0 and zero-width non-joiner (U+200C) for bit 1. Insert between every word in dialogue text. Encode recipientId (128 bits) across the first 64 dialogue words of the script. Extraction: scan dialogue text; collect ZWC sequence; decode UUID. In services/watermark/src/grpc/watermark.service.ts implement: (1) EmbedWatermarkPerRecipient(req: { fileS3Key, fileType: 'pdf'|'fdx', recipientId, outputS3KeyPrefix }): { watermarkedS3Key }. Downloads file from S3; runs embed; uploads watermarked file to S3 at outputS3KeyPrefix/recipientId.pdf|fdx; returns S3 key. (2) ExtractWatermark(req: { fileS3Key, fileType }): { recipientId, confidence }. Expose extraction via REST POST /watermark/extract (multipart upload). Integration test: embed + extract round-trip for both PDF and FDX formats.
Parent: As a distribution manager I can distribute colour-revised scripts with per-recipient invisible watermarks

Title: Create Atlas DDL for call_sheet.call_sheets call_sheet_items crew_assignments tables and deploy call-sheet Temporal saga worker
Description: In packages/db/schema/call_sheet.hcl: (1) call_sheet.call_sheets: id UUID PK, shoot_day_id UUID FK, status ENUM(draft,pending_approval,approved,distributed), approved_by UUID, approved_at TIMESTAMPTZ, distributed_at TIMESTAMPTZ, pdf_s3_key TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. (2) call_sheet.call_sheet_items: id UUID PK, call_sheet_id UUID FK, item_type ENUM(scene,cast,crew,logistics,weather,general_call), content JSONB, sort_order INT. (3) call_sheet.crew_assignments: id UUID PK, call_sheet_id UUID FK, user_id UUID, role TEXT, call_time TIME, report_to TEXT. In workers/call-sheet/: implement CallSheetWorkflow Temporal saga: FetchScheduleActivity (scenes for shoot day), FetchCastActivity (DOOD for cast on day), FetchCrewActivity (crew assignments), GeneratePDFActivity (PDFKit call sheet layout), ADApprovalActivity (signal wait with 4h timeout), DistributeActivity (email per crew assignment). Deploy worker pod. Integration test: run saga for a 3-scene shoot day; assert PDF generated; assert approval signal accepted; assert distribution emails queued.
Parent: Revision Color Workflow and Version Distribution Saga

Title: Create Atlas DDL for supervisor service tables and build take logging and continuity assertion gRPC methods
Description: In packages/db/schema/supervisor.hcl: (1) supervisor.scene_sessions: id UUID PK, script_id UUID, scene_id UUID, shoot_day_id UUID, status ENUM(open,completed), opened_at TIMESTAMPTZ. (2) supervisor.takes: id UUID PK, scene_session_id UUID FK, take_number INT NOT NULL, rating ENUM(print,circle,no_print,hold), notes TEXT, deviation_from_script TEXT, logged_at TIMESTAMPTZ, logged_by UUID. (3) supervisor.continuity_assertions: id UUID PK, scene_session_id UUID FK, subject TEXT NOT NULL, assertion_text TEXT NOT NULL, story_day_ref TEXT, photo_s3_key TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ. (4) supervisor.deviations: id UUID PK, take_id UUID FK, deviation_type ENUM(dialogue,action,prop,wardrobe,location), description TEXT. In services/supervisor/src/grpc/supervisor.service.ts implement: LogTake, RateTake (update rating), CreateContinuityAssertion, GetPresignedUploadURL (S3 presigned PUT URL for photo). NATS publisher: supervisor.take.circled on rating=circle or print; supervisor.scene.completed on session close. Unit tests for all methods.
Parent: As a script supervisor I can log takes and continuity assertions offline during a full shooting day

Title: Build Supervisor Service GraphQL subgraph and NATS event publishing for downstream editorial consumers
Description: In services/supervisor/src/graphql/schema.graphql: type Take { id, takeNumber, rating, notes, deviationFromScript, loggedAt }. type ContinuityAssertion { id, subject, assertionText, storyDayRef, photoS3Key, createdAt }. type SceneSession { id, sceneId, shootDayId, status, takes: [Take!]!, assertions: [ContinuityAssertion!]! }. Extend Scene type: extend type Scene @key(fields: 'id') { takes(sessionId: ID): [Take!]! continuityAssertions: [ContinuityAssertion!]! }. Mutations: logTake, rateTake, createContinuityAssertion, uploadContinuityPhoto (returns presigned URL). Queries: sceneSession(sceneId, shootDayId): SceneSession!. NATS: publish supervisor.take.circled { takeId, sceneId, shootDayId, takeNumber } when rating = circle or print. Publish supervisor.scene.completed { sessionId, sceneId, shootDayId } on session close. Register subgraph. Integration test: log 3 takes; assert NATS event published for the circled take.
Parent: As a script supervisor I can log takes and continuity assertions offline during a full shooting day

Title: Configure PowerSync cloud sync service for supervisor schema and set up role-based data routing for supervisor and AD roles
Description: In infra/powersync/sync-rules.yaml configure PowerSync sync rules. Supervisor role bucket: sync supervisor.takes, supervisor.continuity_assertions, supervisor.scene_sessions WHERE scene_id IN (SELECT scene_id FROM scheduling.scene_schedule_assignments WHERE shoot_day_id = current_shoot_day(userId)). AD role bucket: sync supervisor.takes (read-only), scheduling.shoot_days, call_sheet.call_sheet_items. Data isolation: each role receives only their designated tables and row subsets. Configure PowerSync with connection to Postgres read replica. Test sync: create take in Postgres; assert appears in supervisor-role SQLite device within 10s. Test isolation: assert AD device does not receive continuity_assertions table.
Parent: As a script supervisor I can log takes and continuity assertions offline during a full shooting day

Title: Set up Tauri 2.x on-set app scaffold with SQLite WAL SQLCipher encryption and PowerSync Rust SDK integration
Description: In apps/onset/src-tauri/: initialize Tauri 2.x project with cargo tauri init. Add dependencies to Cargo.toml: tauri 2.x, rusqlite with sqlcipher feature, powersync-rust-sdk (or SQLite extension approach), argon2, tauri-plugin-stronghold. SQLite setup: open DB at app data dir; enable WAL mode (PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL); derive encryption key via Argon2id from session token + device salt; unlock with PRAGMA key. PowerSync Rust SDK: call start_sync(config) on app startup; pass auth token from Tauri secure storage. Implement Tauri commands: sync_status() returns { pendingWrites: number, connected: bool }. React frontend: Vite + React 18 + Tailwind. cargo tauri dev must launch native window with React UI. Verify: open DB file with hex editor; assert first bytes are SQLCipher header (not SQLite plaintext header).
Parent: As a script supervisor I can log takes and continuity assertions offline during a full shooting day

Title: Implement Tauri commands for take logging continuity assertion creation photo capture and sync status indicator in React UI
Description: In apps/onset/src-tauri/src/commands/supervisor.rs implement Tauri commands: #[tauri::command] log_take(scene_id, take_number, notes) → take_id. #[tauri::command] rate_take(take_id, rating: TakeRating). #[tauri::command] create_continuity_assertion(scene_session_id, subject, assertion_text, story_day_ref) → assertion_id. #[tauri::command] attach_continuity_photo(assertion_id, image_bytes): compress to JPEG 85% using image crate; generate thumbnail (200x200); write to SQLite blob + enqueue S3 upload job. #[tauri::command] get_pending_writes_count() → number. In apps/onset/src/: SceneList.tsx (scenes for today's shoot day, from PowerSync SQLite), TakeLogger.tsx (take number counter, rating buttons, notes input, submit → invoke('log_take')), SyncStatusBar.tsx (shows pending count + green/red connectivity indicator). Offline simulation test: disconnect network; log 10 takes; assert all in local SQLite; reconnect; assert all appear in cloud Postgres.
Parent: As a script supervisor I can log takes and continuity assertions offline during a full shooting day

Title: Build revision color badge version history panel and side-by-side diff view in web app
Description: Create apps/web/src/editor/components/RevisionColorBadge.tsx: pill badge with colour name and hex colour matching WGA spec (WHITE=#FFFFFF, BLUE=#ADD8E6, PINK=#FFB6C1, YELLOW=#FFFF99, GREEN=#90EE90, etc.); clickable to open colour change dropdown (only shows valid next colour + current). Create RevisionHistoryPanel.tsx: right panel showing list of script_versions where locked_at IS NOT NULL, sorted desc by lock date; each row shows colour badge + version number + lock date + locked by name + 'View Diff' button. DiffView.tsx: opens as full-page modal; side-by-side layout; left = from version, right = to version; changed scenes highlighted with added text in green background and removed text in red strikethrough; unchanged scenes collapsed with 'N unchanged scenes' expand button. Data: useScriptVersionsQuery + useScriptVersionDiffQuery. Vitest test: render DiffView with 1 added scene and 1 modified scene; assert correct highlight classes applied.
Parent: As a writer I can track coloured revision pages following the WGA standard sequence and compare any two locked versions

Title: Build call sheet viewer component and watermark export modal with recipient warning
Description: Create apps/web/src/call-sheet/components/CallSheetViewer.tsx: render call sheet in print-ready layout (header: production name, shoot date, call time; sections: scene breakdown table, cast call times, crew call times, logistics, notes). Data from useCallSheetQuery(callSheetId). Print button triggers window.print() with call sheet CSS media print styles. Create WatermarkExportModal.tsx: opened when user clicks 'Export Script' on any published version. Shows recipient selector (multi-select of distribution list users), format selector (PDF, FDX), 'Watermark will be embedded per recipient' amber notice, confirm button triggers mutation startRevisionDistribution(scriptVersionId, recipientIds). Vitest test: render WatermarkExportModal; select 3 recipients; assert startRevisionDistribution called with 3 recipientIds.
Parent: As a distribution manager I can distribute colour-revised scripts with per-recipient invisible watermarks
